claim: The frozen legacy baseline contains 34 route records with title description H1 navigation visible-text and DOM signatures
excerpt_or_observation: legacy-freeze-manifest.json reports schemaVersion legacy-freeze-v2 and 34 page entries with route-level signature fields
counter_evidence: A manifest does not by itself prove every future release still matches it
review_notes: Worker direct observation; curator must independently rerun the freeze gate

claim: Additive related tutorial blocks are registered on nine specific legacy routes rather than all legacy pages
excerpt_or_observation: The registry has nine route keys: guides 0 1 2 3 4 5 and 6, more 03-esim, and answers
counter_evidence: Registration does not prove the rendered release has no duplicate or misplaced slot
review_notes: Append-only opportunity is bounded to the approved registry; no new slot was proposed

claim: The release contract strips only the named additive related-tutorial and commerce-widget sections before comparing frozen legacy signatures
excerpt_or_observation: withoutGrowthSlot removes data-growth-slot related-tutorials-v1 and wechat-buying-guide-v1; the test then compares title description H1 navigation visible copy and legacy DOM to the freeze manifest
counter_evidence: Passing source logic still requires an actual test run on the candidate artifact
review_notes: This supports append-only architecture, not permission to edit frozen body or navigation

claim: Submitting a sitemap to Google is a hint and does not guarantee download or use for crawling
excerpt_or_observation: Google states: submitting a sitemap is merely a hint; it does not guarantee Google will download the sitemap or use it for crawling site URLs
counter_evidence: A verified GSC property can still provide useful fetch and processing readback
review_notes: Corroborates IndexNow receipt boundary but does not report getgiffgaff property state

claim: The GSC Sitemaps report requires owner permission to submit and reports submission history fetch status parsing errors and discovered pages
excerpt_or_observation: Official help says owner permissions are required to submit in the report; it exposes last request status, parsing errors and discovered pages, and warns not all sitemap URLs may be crawled
counter_evidence: A sitemap can also be advertised through robots.txt without using this report
review_notes: Use this to define the required dated backend export, not as proof of current ingestion

claim: An IndexNow HTTP 200 response means the engine received the URL or URL set, not that it crawled or indexed it
excerpt_or_observation: Protocol documentation says the HTTP 200 response code only indicates the search engine has received the URL or set of URLs
counter_evidence: A later engine dashboard may separately show receipt crawling or indexing state
review_notes: Corroborates Google's submission boundary at the general measurement level

claim: Bing's IndexNow setup requires a key hosted on the site submission of changed URLs and verification in Bing Webmaster Tools
excerpt_or_observation: Microsoft lists four steps: generate API key, host key, submit URLs, then use Bing Webmaster Tools to verify URLs were received
counter_evidence: Receipt in Bing Webmaster Tools still does not establish indexation or ranking
review_notes: No IndexNow request was sent in this worker run

claim: The repository records that 39 canonical URLs received an IndexNow HTTP 202 on 2026-07-16
excerpt_or_observation: Runbook line 127 records a 39-URL submission to api.indexnow.org with HTTP 202 and states this only means batch received with key validation pending
counter_evidence: No current Bing IndexNow Insights export was supplied to show later validation crawl or index state
review_notes: Historical repository record; curator should require raw receipt or backend export before upgrading confidence

claim: Current GSC and Bing sitemap ingestion for the 39 URLs cannot be verified from public pages without an authorized property readback
excerpt_or_observation: The public request redirected to authentication; no authorized screenshot export or property session was supplied and the worker did not log in
counter_evidence: Public sitemap and IndexNow receipt establish discoverability inputs but not backend ingestion
review_notes: Blocked card; authorized owner should export dated sitemap and URL inspection results

claim: Current Bing sitemap and IndexNow Insights state cannot be verified without an authorized Bing Webmaster Tools readback
excerpt_or_observation: The public request redirected to authentication; no authorized screenshot or export was supplied and the worker did not log in
counter_evidence: The repository records only an earlier protocol receipt, not current engine processing
review_notes: Blocked card; authorized owner should export sitemap status and IndexNow Insights

claim: Baidu normal inclusion can shorten discovery and crawling time but cannot resolve whether content is indexed
excerpt_or_observation: Official page says the tool can shorten link discovery and speed crawling, and explicitly says it cannot solve whether site content is included
counter_evidence: A verified property may expose additional diagnostics after submission
review_notes: No Baidu submission was performed

claim: Baidu site management asks users to add a site and verify it to obtain domain ownership
excerpt_or_observation: Public page says to add a website and that verification can obtain domain ownership and enable adding sub-sites and viewing data
counter_evidence: The public prompt does not prove getgiffgaff has completed ownership verification
review_notes: Owner authentication steps remain outside this worker scope

claim: Baidu's robots tool describes robots.txt as the control for which site pages Baidu may or may not crawl
excerpt_or_observation: Official page says robots.txt tells Baidu which pages can and cannot be crawled and its tool can create validate update and inspect effect
counter_evidence: Allowing crawl does not guarantee crawl inclusion ranking or traffic
review_notes: Crawler rule semantics should be checked separately from submission state

claim: The current ownership submission crawl and inclusion state of getgiffgaff in Baidu is not evidenced by an authorized backend export
excerpt_or_observation: No dated property screenshot export or authorized session was supplied; the worker did not attempt login real-name verification phone verification or OTP
counter_evidence: Public Baidu documentation is accessible but cannot establish property-specific completion
review_notes: Blocked card; only the authorized owner can close with dated property evidence

claim: Current Core Web Vitals targets are LCP at most 2.5 seconds INP at most 200 milliseconds and CLS at most 0.1 at the 75th percentile
excerpt_or_observation: web.dev lists LCP within 2.5s, INP 200ms or less, CLS 0.1 or less, evaluated at the 75th percentile segmented by mobile and desktop
counter_evidence: Thresholds alone provide no measurements for getgiffgaff
review_notes: Use only as evaluation criteria

claim: Field data describes real-user experience while lab data is diagnostic and Lighthouse is not a substitute for field data
excerpt_or_observation: Official guidance says field data describes what real users experienced, CrUX is a 28-day field dataset when sufficiently sampled, and Lighthouse is primarily diagnostic rather than a field-data substitute
counter_evidence: A controlled lab run can still expose reproducible regressions and opportunities
review_notes: This prevents treating the lab score as a CWV pass

claim: The public mobile PageSpeed report had no CrUX field data for getgiffgaff at capture time
excerpt_or_observation: The report's real-user section displayed No Data
counter_evidence: No Data does not mean good poor or zero traffic; private RUM or Search Console may contain other evidence
review_notes: SG-17 remains open because a field CWV dataset is still unavailable

claim: One mobile Lighthouse run scored Performance 100 Accessibility 96 Best Practices 100 and SEO 100 with LCP 0.9s TBT 0ms and CLS 0
excerpt_or_observation: Report used Lighthouse 13.4.0 HeadlessChromium 149 emulated Moto G Power and Slow 4G; it also flagged insufficient contrast and one long main-thread task
counter_evidence: Values are estimated and may vary; the run has no INP field measurement and accessibility automation is incomplete
review_notes: Diagnostic signal only; do not call this a field CWV pass

claim: Current Search Console Core Web Vitals URL-group evidence was not available to the worker
excerpt_or_observation: No authorized property session or dated export was supplied and the worker did not log in
counter_evidence: The public PSI report independently exposed a no-data field state but cannot replace property URL-group history
review_notes: Blocked card; authorized owner should export mobile and desktop groups with dates

claim: The production G0 product page routes both visible purchase actions to the generic contact page rather than a G0-specific external SKU checkout
excerpt_or_observation: Direct HTML observation found the purchase and WeChat actions point to /contact/; the only external contact channel in the additive widget is the WeChat link
counter_evidence: A QR image may lead to a usable mini-program after a real-device scan, which was not tested
review_notes: No payment or external action performed

claim: The production G2 product page routes both visible purchase actions to the same generic contact page rather than a G2-specific external SKU checkout
excerpt_or_observation: Direct HTML observation found the purchase and WeChat actions point to /contact/; no product-specific external checkout URL appears in the page HTML
counter_evidence: A QR image may resolve to a product flow on a real device, which was not tested
review_notes: Separate SKU identity and real payment evidence remain required

claim: The contact page uses the same ktt-giga-card fragment for G0 and G2 inventory confirmation and contains no direct external Kuaijiantuan SKU URL
excerpt_or_observation: Both G0 and G2 buttons link to #ktt-giga-card; public external hrefs were the WeChat contact and giffgaff help, not separate G0 or G2 checkout URLs
counter_evidence: The image payload could contain a usable mini-program code but same-device and payment behavior require real-device testing
review_notes: Direct observation supports the current task-completion blocker

claim: The production order guide still includes action-oriented text about obtaining an eSIM QR code and writing it into 9eSIM
excerpt_or_observation: Direct HTML text search returned both 9eSIM and writing language on the legacy navigation surface
counter_evidence: The dedicated safety page rejects credential extraction and upload steps
review_notes: Requires approved narrow legacy exception; append-only copy cannot remove the conflicting action text

claim: The additive commerce widget visibly states independent third-party status G0/G2 seller-defined scope no inventory payment or OTP guarantee and a sensitive-credential warning
excerpt_or_observation: Static markup says the site does not represent giffgaff, G0/G2 are inventory classifications, and users must not send passwords SMS codes or full card information
counter_evidence: The legacy body can make stronger or conflicting claims outside this additive widget
review_notes: Disclosure is useful but does not close business-evidence or policy gaps

claim: The additive purchase dialog implements modal open and close focus placement Escape cancellation backdrop close Tab trapping and opener focus return
excerpt_or_observation: Direct source inspection found showModal fallback aria-modal management cancel and keydown listeners focusable cycling and lastOpener focus restoration
counter_evidence: Source inspection does not replace assistive-technology testing across browser and device combinations
review_notes: Supports keyboard task completion narrowly

claim: The production privacy route returns HTTP 404
excerpt_or_observation: Direct curl observation returned HTTP 404
counter_evidence: Privacy notes exist on some individual widgets but are not a consolidated policy
review_notes: Do not fill with invented business practices

claim: The production terms route returns HTTP 404
excerpt_or_observation: Direct curl observation returned HTTP 404
counter_evidence: Carrier terms links do not substitute for the seller's own transaction terms
review_notes: Do not fill with invented business practices

claim: The production refund route returns HTTP 404
excerpt_or_observation: Direct curl observation returned HTTP 404
counter_evidence: Individual customer-support statements do not establish a reviewed refund policy
review_notes: Do not infer return eligibility timing or fees

claim: The production shipping route returns HTTP 404
excerpt_or_observation: Direct curl observation returned HTTP 404
counter_evidence: Legacy pages contain delivery statements but not a consolidated evidence-backed shipping policy
review_notes: Do not infer carrier dispatch SLA loss handling or regions

claim: All eight growth manifest entries carry updatedAt and reviewedAt 2026-07-17 but the manifest has no claim-level owner expiresAt or revision-log fields
excerpt_or_observation: Direct parse found 8 pages, 5 index and 3 noindex, with one shared reviewedAt and updatedAt date; no claim owner expiry or revision log fields are present
counter_evidence: The source registry and page copy contain some inline review and expiry information outside the manifest
review_notes: Supports SG-19 remaining open

claim: The growth source registry defines one review date and one 2026-08-15 expiry used by the keep-number and China roaming tools
excerpt_or_observation: REVIEWED_AT is 2026-07-17 and EXPIRES_AT is 2026-08-15; data-expires is attached to keep-number and roaming-cost tools
counter_evidence: Other dynamic claims about inventory balance shipping plans and compatibility do not share this executable expiry gate
review_notes: Useful partial freshness control, not a complete claim registry

claim: The tool library returns no current result when the expiry is invalid missing or earlier than the supplied current date
excerpt_or_observation: evidenceIsCurrent requires valid ISO dates and current <= expiry; roamingCostBreakdown returns null when this check fails
counter_evidence: Static explanatory copy remains visible after expiry and still requires editorial review
review_notes: Fail-closed applies to calculated output, not all page claims

claim: A regression test asserts that China roaming breakdown and total return null on 2026-08-16 after the configured 2026-08-15 expiry
excerpt_or_observation: The test named roaming breakdown remains fail-closed after evidence expiry supplies now 2026-08-16 and asserts null for both APIs
counter_evidence: The test source still needs execution in the final integrated candidate
review_notes: Curator should confirm the test passes without weakening the date

claim: The current repository does not contain a complete claim-level expiry registry covering owner source checked date expiry behavior and revision history
excerpt_or_observation: Direct inspection found page-level source arrays and shared dates plus two executable expiry uses, but no complete per-claim owner expiresAt fail-closed mode or revision-log structure
counter_evidence: Some page sections do carry near-claim official citations and review dates, and two tools fail closed
review_notes: Do not close SG-19 until a complete registry artifact exists
